steps.title: Name the tested relationship
steps.content: Hensley et al. tested whether perceived category similarity predicts the chance that consumers buy brand extensions. They measured similarity ratings and the change in purchase probability for each extended-brand pair.

steps.title: Reject unrelated findings
steps.content: A introduces brand recognition as a confound. C says ratings vary while probability changes do not, weakening prediction. D compares categories with and without extensions, not similarity ratings to purchase likelihood. A positive relationship between the two measured values would support the possibility. The answer is B.
